content.problemStatement:
Given an array AAA of size NNN, find the size of the largest subarray of AAA which has an **even sum**.

For example, for the array [2,1,2][2, 1, 2][2,1,2] the answer is just  111 as the largest subarray with an even sum is [2][2][2].

Note that a subarray is a contiguous part of an array. For the array [1,3,2][1, 3, 2][1,3,2], some possible subarrays are [1],[2],[1,3],[1,3,2][1], [2], [1,3], [1,3,2][1],[2],[1,3],[1,3,2]. However, [1,2][1,2][1,2] is not a subarray for this array.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN — the size of the array.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integer - A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the size of the largest subarray with even sum.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the size of the array. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1 … AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single integer on its own line – the maximum length of a contiguous subarray whose sum is even.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: total sum = 5 (odd). First odd at index 1, last odd at index 1. Removing either side leaves length 1. Test case 2: total sum = 6 (even) → length 3. Test case 3: total sum = 9 (odd). First odd index 2, last odd index 4. Removing prefix up to index 2 gives length 3, removing suffix from index 4 gives length 4 → maximum 4. Test case 4: single odd element → no even‑sum subarray, answer 0.
First array all even, total sum even → answer 5. Second array sum = 15 (odd). First odd at 0, last odd at 4. Removing one element from either end leaves length 4, which is maximal.
Total sum = 21 (odd). First odd index 0, last odd index 4. Removing the first element gives subarray of length 5 with even sum.

solution.approach: Let S be the sum of the whole array. If S is even, the whole array is a valid subarray and the answer is N. If S is odd, we must discard at least one element with odd value because removing an even element does not change parity. To keep the subarray as long as possible we drop the smallest possible prefix or suffix that contains an odd element. Let left be the index of the first odd element (0‑based) and right be the index of the last odd element. Removing the prefix up to left gives length N‑(left+1). Removing the suffix from right to the end gives length right. The best we can do is the larger of these two lengths, which equals N‑min(left+1, N‑right). If the array has length 1 and the only element is odd, the answer becomes 0. The algorithm scans the array once to compute the total sum and the positions of the first and last odd numbers, yielding O(N) time and O(1) extra space per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(T·N) overall.
